title: Dryopteris ingens
taxonomy: Dryopteris ingens is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of large, terrestrial fern native to parts of Central and South America. The genus Dryopteris is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. This classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics of Dryopteris ingens within the broader context of plant taxonomy.
uses: Dryopteris ingens is a species of fern native to New Zealand, and it has been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes.
